The recorded session + root cause combination is what makes this interesting. finding that something broke is useful, but giving a coding agent the exact interaction, state, and context that caused it could remove a huge amount of back-and-forth between QA and engineering.

The first-time experience also sounds almost suspiciously fast :)) Curious how Replay QA handles flaky or environment-specific behavior. can it tell the difference between a real product bug, a temporary network issue, and something that only breaks under one browser or session state?

results.title: Replay QA: Catching App Breaks Before Users Do | Replay.io posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
Most AI-built apps ship without a test suite. There's no QA person catching what slips through.
So the first honest test the app gets is a real user, and the first you hear about a broken flow is when they complain. Or when they quietly stop using it.
Replay QA changes that. Give it a URL, and it explores your app like a real user would: clicking through flows, filling forms, trying checkout. It files real bugs, each with a root cause and the recording that backs it up. [...] Most AI-built apps ship without a test suite. There's no QA person catching what slips through.
So the first honest test the app gets is a real user, and the first you hear about a broken flow is when they complain. Or when they quietly stop using it.
Replay QA changes that. Give it a URL, and it explores your app like a real user would: clicking through flows, filling forms, trying checkout. It files real bugs, each with a root cause and the recording that backs it up. [...] The recording is what makes it worth acting on. Other tools hand you a screenshot. A screenshot shows you the app froze \- it doesn't show you what led up to it. Replay captures the whole session, so you can replay the exact moment something broke. If you're building with a coding agent, that recording is what the agent needs to fix the bug, rather than hallucinate a solution.
First run takes a few minutes. Runs in the background.

results.title: Replay QA — AI wrote the app. Replay QA finds what broke.
results.content:
The result: AI-built apps ship without meaningful test coverage. Internal tools get deployed with no QA layer at all. And the bugs your users hit are the first signal that something is wrong.

Replay QA gives AI-built apps the QA loop they're missing.

Two ways to run it

## Add your GitHub repo, or drop in a URL

Same autonomous QA, two ways to start — a continuous quality gate for teams, or a one-time check for whatever you just shipped.

Continuously-running

### Connect your GitHub repo

A quality gate that stays on as your team ships. Add your repo, authenticate with GitHub, and the Replay QA app installs — no test suite, no config. Then choose when it runs. [...] Learn more about how Replay QA works

Also available

## Use Replay QA wherever AI is shipping code

### Replay for CI

Already running Playwright or Cypress? Replay QA records every test run, analyzes every failure, and posts root cause and fix to your PR automatically.

Learn more →

### Replay QA API

Building a software factory or AI coding platform? Embed Replay QA as a quality gate via API. Every app your system generates gets tested before it ships.

Talk to us →

## Built on technology teams already trust

Replay QA runs on the same recording engine used every day by teams at Vercel, Glide, Pantheon, and more.

> “Before Replay, we spent somewhere between 1–2 hours per day per dev in this reproducibility purgatory.”

Mark Probst

VP Engineering, Glide [...] Replay QA

# AI wrote the app. Replay QA finds what broke.

Add your GitHub repo for continuous testing, or drop in a URL for a one-time check. Replay QA explores your app, records every session, finds real bugs, and gives your coding agent the root cause and fix.

Replay is trusted by top engineering teams

## Apps ship faster than ever. QA hasn't kept up.

AI coding tools have compressed development cycles from weeks to hours. A solo founder or a small team can now ship a full web app in a day. But the way we test software hasn't changed — it still requires engineers to write test suites, QA teams to run them, and someone to triage what breaks.

results.title: How Replay QA Works
results.content:
02

### Authenticate with GitHub

Sign in with your GitHub account. One technical lead can complete setup — no procurement, no engineering ticket.

03

### The Replay QA app installs

The GitHub app installs on your repo. No existing Playwright suite, no playwright.config.ts changes, no separate bot to wire up.

04

### Choose your run cadence

Run on every main-branch update, on every pull request, or both. On PRs, root cause and a suggested fix are posted right on the pull request.

Connect your GitHub repo

What you get

## Root cause. Suggested fix. Full recording.

Every bug Replay QA finds comes with everything your agent needs to fix it — not just “something broke.”

Bug found

confidence: high

Checkout button does nothing on mobile Safari

Root cause [...] ## Underneath it all: our time-travel debugger.

Whether you're running Replay QA on a web app or analyzing a CI failure, the same recording engine is at work. Replay captures the full browser runtime deterministically — making AI analysis possible where it wasn't before.

Render chainsWhich component re-rendered, what triggered it, what changed

State flowRedux actions, React state updates, context changes across the component tree

Network timingEvery request and response, with exact payload and timing data

JS executionEvery function call on every frame, with arguments and return values

DOM mutationsWhat changed in the DOM, when, and what code caused it

This isn't guessing from error messages. It's reading the actual execution.

## Test your app for free. [...] How it works

# From URL to bug report. Automatically.

Give Replay QA a URL. It explores your app, records every session with our time-travel debugger, and delivers a root cause and suggested fix for every bug it finds.

How it works

## From URL to bug report — automatically

Testing continuously

## Connect your GitHub repo

The URL drop is a run-it-once check. When you want a quality gate that stays on as your team ships, connect a repo instead — same autonomous QA, running on every change. No test suite, no pipeline config.

01

### Add your repo URL

Paste a link to your GitHub repository — the same “paste a link to your thing” pattern as the URL drop.

02

### Authenticate with GitHub

results.content:
🎉 GoReplay is now part of Probe Labs. 🎉

GoReplay Logo

# Replay Your Production Traffic with GoReplayThe Ultimate Traffic Replay Testing Tool

GoReplay empowers you to capture and replay real production traffic, transforming live user interactions into powerful tests. Our traffic replay tool enables you to perform replay testing, catching issues before they impact your users. Deploy with confidence knowing that GoReplay has thoroughly vetted your applications under real-world conditions.

`$ gor --input-raw :8080 --output-http="

Why GoReplay?

## Reality-Driven Testing

Because in production, there's no such thing as a "typical" user.
Test with real traffic, real patterns, and get real confidence.

### Session-Aware Replay [...] ### Session-Aware Replay

Replay production traffic with true session boundaries, connection pooling, and optimized TLS handling that matches real user patterns.

### Load Testing

Stop guessing if your app can handle the load. Know it can, with real-world traffic patterns.

### Monitoring & Analytics

See your application through your users' eyes. Every request, every pattern, every insight.

## Simplicity Meets Power

Production-grade testing shouldn't require a PhD. Three steps to unshakeable confidence.

### Capture Reality

Your users write your test cases. Every click, every interaction, captured without impact.

### Shape the Flow

Filter, transform, and direct traffic exactly where you need it. Your rules, your way.

### Know, Don't Hope
